methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $96M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Arbor Energy is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Brad Hartwig. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 same-stage, same-sector private peers (Graphyte, Fourth Power, Steady Energy) alongside a broader set of 8 private climate-tech companies and 1 public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses total raised to date ($96M) as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $192M. The peer-median method anchors to the median valuation across the 3 direct comparables at $150M, with a range from $110M to $200M. These peers represent established trajectory and capital structures in the sector.

The triangulated analysis yields a median implied valuation of $171M, with a low bound of $128M and high bound of $221M. The median reflects roughly 1.8x the capital deployed to date. Key uncertainties include the timing of the next financing event, margin assumptions relative to peer trajectory, and whether regulatory or technology risk merits a valuation discount within the peer range. The $171M median sits appropriately between the stage-progression and peer-median anchors and warrants consideration as a fair-value reference point for Series A pricing.
